_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast basket, an average price index of coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice set to 100 in 2015, currently stands at 141, indicating a 41% increase since 2015. This rise reflects uneven and recent price movements across the components rather than a uniform trend. The index aggregates global commodity prices to offer a composite view of cost changes for common breakfast staples. The increase is notable but concentrated in select commodities rather than broad-based inflation.
notes.movers: Coffee prices have surged 99% since 2015, making it the primary driver behind the basket's increase. Orange juice is up 34%, wheat 19%, and sugar 13%, contributing to the overall rise but to a lesser extent. These shifts result largely from weather disruptions and crop diseases affecting thin soft commodity markets, such as frost and drought impacting coffee-growing regions and hurricanes/citrus greening affecting orange juice production. This indicates supply-side volatility rather than general inflationary pressures.
notes.why: The headline rise in the breakfast basket is driven by a few weather-sensitive crops vulnerable to environmental factors, emphasizing the often volatile nature of soft commodity prices. Due to the limited depth of these markets and ongoing climate uncertainties, elevated price inputs in these commodities could persist over multiple years.
